title: Warhammer Quest - Take a Look - Part 15 - Fan Created and DIY stuff
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f8fN8Uqbb8s

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into the vast world of fan-created Warhammer Quest content, arguing this is where the game truly shines. The star of the community is Littlemonk — the internet's leading champion of the game, maintaining the definitive Warhammer Quest Customized Runboard with expansions, warriors, scenarios, and missions.

Daniel tours key resources: the WQ Chronicles website with its d100 event tables, Eastern Empire's quest library, the Lost and the Damned community, and the Warhammer Quest Museum. He then examines his printed DIY collection — books of d100 dungeon events, travelling hazards, settlement events, expanded alehouses, and gambling houses. Littlemonk's "Beyond the Grey Mountains" campaign (ambitious but unfinished) introduced physical and mental injury systems, colour-coded settlement cards, and thematic event decks.

The crown jewel is Littlemonk's Hall of the Hag Queen — a full expansion rivalling the official boxed sets, complete with new board pieces (bottomless pit, crystal cave, water cave), a Dark Elf objective room, monster tables, a Games Master adventure called "The Slavery Coast," and presentation indistinguishable from official GW products. Daniel also showcases the Ultimate Adventure Book compiling every known quest, plus Littlemonk's travelling hazards and settlement events compilations. The DIY community has ensured Warhammer Quest will never truly die.

----

Have fan expansions ever elevated a game beyond what the original publisher achieved?
